title: Rich Fluffy Egyptian Wild Garlic Leaves Tagine
description: A romantic Egyptian dish featuring wild garlic leaves, this rich and fluffy tagine is perfect for a special occasion. It's also FODMAP-friendly and cooked to perfection using an air fryer.
instructions: Rinse the wild garlic leaves and pat them dry with a paper towel.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic-infused oil, salt, black pepper, cumin powder, and coriander powder.
Add the chicken breast to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the marinade and cook in the air fryer for 10-12 minutes, or until cooked through.
Add the wild garlic leaves to the air fryer and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until wilted.
In a small bowl, whisk together chicken broth and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Pour the chicken broth mixture over the cooked chicken and wild garlic leaves.
Stir everything together and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Serve hot, garnished with additional wild garlic leaves if desired.
